description: Organised crime in India encompasses various criminal groups operating across the country and internationally, involved in activities such as smuggling, extortion, drug trafficking, and contract killing. The Mumbai underworld has been historically dominated by figures like Haji Mastan, Varadarajan Mudaliar, and Karim Lala. These groups have expanded their operations to countries including Canada, the United States, and the United Kingdom. The structure and leadership of Indian organized crime do not strictly follow the classical Sicilian Mafia model.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: India, Pakistan , Afghanistan , Nepal , Bangladesh , Sri Lanka , Canada , United Kingdom , Australia , United States , Mauritius , Iran , Malaysia , Singapore , Kuwait , Qatar , UAE , Oman , Saudi Arabia , Jordan , Indonesia , Nigeria , South Africa , Somalia , Fiji , Guyana
country_links.context: The organization has territory in Afghanistan.
